questions.question: In a right triangle, the side opposite a 35° angle is 8 cm and the hypotenuse is 14 cm. Find the sine of the 35° angle, rounded to the nearest hundredth.
questions.explanation: By definition, sin(35°) = opposite/hypotenuse = 8/14 ≈ 0.571, which rounds to 0.57.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall, forming a 60° angle with the ground. If the ladder is 10 meters long, how far up the wall does it reach? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 60° angle: height = 10 × sin(60°) = 10 × (√3/2) ≈ 8.66 ≈ 8.7 m.

questions.question: What is cos(90°)?
questions.explanation: At 90° on the unit circle, the point is (0, 1), so cosine (the x-coordinate) equals 0.

questions.question: In a right triangle, one acute angle measures 28° and the adjacent side to that angle is 12 units. Find the length of the hypotenuse, rounded to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: Using cosine: cos(28°) = adjacent/hypotenuse = 12/h, so h = 12/cos(28°) ≈ 12/0.883 ≈ 13.6 ≈ 13.

questions.question: Evaluate: sin²(45°) + cos²(45°)
questions.explanation: This is the Pythagorean identity: sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1 for all angles θ.

questions.question: If tan(θ) = 5/12 and θ is an acute angle in a right triangle, what is sin(θ)?
questions.explanation: Tan(θ) = opposite/adjacent = 5/12, so opposite = 5 and adjacent = 12. Hypotenuse = √(5² + 12²) = 13. Thus sin(θ) = 5/13.
